category_title: Low-attaining students who use physical measurement with manipulatives to identify fractional parts
minimal_context: David is working individually with Cuisenaire rods to identify quarters during a Year 5 mathematics lesson.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here are the red and brown Cuisenaire rods. Please use them to identify quarters.

STUDENT: (Physically placing four red rods against a brown rod) ‘Cause there’s four pieces.

TUTOR: So how do you know it’s a quarter?

STUDENT: (Begins to use the red rods and places them beside the brown rod) See if it’s a quarter or not.

TUTOR: What are you checking to see?

STUDENT: (Moving small red rods along the longer rod) What it is. I’m kind of measuring it.

options.A: Give a warning at the time but do not discipline the student further during the activity.
options.B: Rephrase and extend the student's contribution to make the underlying mathematical rule explicit during the activity.
options.C: Ask an open-ended question about how the student got the answer or what they will do next.
options.D: Allow the student to withdraw from the environment for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
